name: Apple Inc.
description: Apple the tech company — products, founders, and history

questions.q: In which US state does Apple have its primary headquarters?

source.notes: Based in Cupertino, California.

questions.q: What is the name of Apple's voice-activated virtual assistant?

source.notes: Launched with the iPhone 4S in October 2011.

questions.q: Apple's touchscreen tablet is called the what?

source.notes: First generation launched April 3, 2010.

questions.q: What was the name of Apple's iconic portable music player, launched in 2001?

source.notes: Discontinued in 2022.

questions.q: What is the name of the mobile operating system that runs on iPhones?

source.notes: Originally called 'iPhone OS' until June 2010.

questions.q: What is the brand name Apple uses for its laptop computers?

source.notes: Includes the MacBook Air and MacBook Pro.

questions.q: What is the name of Apple's music streaming service, launched in 2015?

source.notes: Developed after Apple acquired Beats Electronics in 2014.

questions.q: In what year was the first iPad released?

source.notes: Launched by Steve Jobs in January 2010; on sale April 3, 2010.

questions.q: Apple's App Store, selling third-party apps for the iPhone, launched in which year?

source.notes: Launched July 10, 2008 with about 500 apps.

questions.q: The first iPhone was released in which year?

source.notes: Announced January 9, 2007; on sale June 29, 2007.

questions.q: Which Mac OS X version is named after a famous U.S. national park?

source.notes: OS X 10.10 'Yosemite' released October 2014.

questions.q: In 1997, which Microsoft co-founder controversially invested $150 million in the then-struggling Apple?

source.notes: Part of a settlement that included a software-sharing agreement.

questions.q: Apple's iconic 1984 Macintosh commercial aired during which annual US sporting event?

source.notes: Directed by Ridley Scott; aired during Super Bowl XVIII.

questions.q: Apple's famous glass-cube flagship retail store is on which iconic New York City street?

source.notes: Located at 767 Fifth Avenue; the cube is 32 feet on each side.

questions.q: In what year did Apple drop the colorful rainbow apple logo in favor of a monochrome design?

source.notes: Replaced with a translucent/chrome 'aqua' style and later a flat black/white logo.

questions.q: Apple's spaceship-shaped headquarters, opened in 2017, is known by what name?

source.notes: Designed by Foster + Partners; houses 12,000+ employees.

questions.q: Steve Jobs returned to Apple in 1997 after Apple acquired which company that he had co-founded?

source.notes: NeXT's NeXTSTEP OS became the foundation of Mac OS X.

questions.q: The iPod's name is said to have been inspired by a line from which classic sci-fi film?

source.notes: 'Open the pod bay doors, HAL' — said to have inspired the name.

questions.q: Apple is headquartered in which Silicon Valley city?

source.notes: One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, CA.

questions.q: Who was the often-forgotten third co-founder of Apple, alongside Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak?

source.notes: Sold his 10% stake for just $800 in 1976.

questions.q: The Macintosh computer line was named after what type of apple?

source.notes: A North American apple variety; a favorite of Apple employee Jef Raskin.

questions.q: In which year did Apple become the first U.S.-listed company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization?

source.notes: Hit $1T on August 2, 2018.
